read.thesis: Salesforce presents a compelling value proposition with its EV/EBITDA multiple trading 25% below its 10-year median, attracting smart-money initiation from Chuck Akre. This valuation discount is coupled with strong fundamental momentum, evidenced by 44 upward EPS revisions in the last 30 days and a recent upgrade to Buy by Guggenheim.
read.risk: The primary risk is that the 38% YTD decline reflects structural growth deceleration or margin compression that justifies the lower multiple, turning the current valuation into a value trap.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings prints for sustained EPS growth and management commentary on margin expansion to confirm the fundamental momentum supporting the upward revisions.

read.thesis: Domino's Pizza offers a deep value setup, trading at a P/E of 21.0 (34% below its historical median), which has drawn smart-money initiation from Eric Mandelblatt and congressional buying. The combination of this discounted valuation and a crowded short interest (15% of float, 5.9 days to cover) creates a potent setup for a short squeeze if fundamentals stabilize.
read.risk: The bear case centers on persistent consumer weakness or increased competition eroding market share, which could validate the short thesis and prevent any multiple expansion.
read.watch: Watch for the next quarterly earnings guide regarding same-store sales and franchisee profitability to trigger short covering or validate the bearish sentiment.

read.thesis: H&R Block is trading at a significant 46% discount to its 10-year median P/S, prompting smart-money initiation from Li Lu. This deep value is supported by positive EPS revisions and a highly crowded short position (25% of float, 8.0 days to cover), setting the stage for a potential squeeze on any positive catalyst.
read.risk: The strongest risk is that the core tax preparation business is facing secular decline or disruption from free alternatives, justifying the low multiple and high short interest.
read.watch: Look for the upcoming earnings report and forward guidance on tax season volumes to either ignite a short squeeze or confirm the structural headwinds.

signals.label: 4 super-investors
signals.detail: Bill Ackman added +0%; Seth Klarman added +0%; David Tepper added +1%

read.thesis: Amazon exhibits strong institutional and congressional confidence, with additions from Ackman, Klarman, and Tepper, alongside net congressional buying. This bullish sentiment is fundamentally backed by a 12% increase in FY EPS estimates over 90 days and heavily skewed call options flow ($21M calls vs $6M puts), indicating expectations of continued upside.
read.risk: A significant risk is that the heavy call options flow and high expectations leave the stock vulnerable to a sharp pullback if AWS growth or retail margins disappoint in the near term.
read.watch: Monitor the next earnings release, specifically AWS revenue growth and operating margin expansion, to validate the aggressive upward EPS revisions and options positioning.

signals.label: 2 super-investors
signals.detail: David Tepper initiated; Stanley Druckenmiller initiated

read.thesis: Sandisk shows explosive fundamental momentum with FY EPS estimates surging 63% over 90 days, attracting high-profile initiations from Tepper and Druckenmiller. Despite a massive 663% YTD run, the stock still has a 12% short interest, providing additional fuel for upside if the fundamental trajectory continues.
read.risk: The primary risk is that the massive YTD outperformance has fully priced in the fundamental improvements, making the stock highly susceptible to a cyclical downturn in the memory market.
read.watch: Watch for follow-through buying from institutions and the next earnings guide to confirm that the aggressive EPS revisions are sustainable and not a cyclical peak.

read.thesis: ServiceNow is trading at a 28% discount to its historical P/S median, creating an attractive entry point that has drawn smart-money initiation from Chuck Akre and net congressional buying. This valuation support is reinforced by a recent upgrade to Buy from Guggenheim, suggesting the 32% YTD decline may be overdone.
read.risk: The bear case is that the historical P/S multiple is no longer appropriate due to slowing enterprise IT spending, meaning the stock could still be expensive despite the relative discount.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings for enterprise deal closures and forward guidance on IT budget environments to confirm the Guggenheim upgrade and value thesis.

read.thesis: Cleveland-Cliffs is trading at a deep 64% discount to its 10-year median P/S, attracting additions from Stanley Druckenmiller. This extreme value, combined with positive EPS revisions and a 14% short interest, creates a compelling setup for a value-driven short squeeze.
read.risk: The risk is that the low P/S multiple reflects a structural deterioration in steel demand or pricing power, making the stock a cyclical value trap rather than a bargain.
read.watch: Watch for the next earnings print and management commentary on steel pricing and demand trends to either trigger short covering or validate the depressed valuation.

signals.label: 2 super-investors
signals.detail: Chuck Akre added +0%; Investment Committee initiated

read.thesis: Roper Technologies presents a strong value case, trading at a P/E 40% below its 10-year median, which has prompted additions from Chuck Akre and the Investment Committee. This valuation discount is supported by consistent upward EPS revisions (17 up, 0 down in 30 days), indicating underlying fundamental stability despite the 19% YTD decline.
read.risk: The strongest bear case is that the historical multiple was inflated by an acquisition-heavy growth strategy that is no longer viable in a higher interest rate environment.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings for organic growth metrics and capital allocation updates to confirm the fundamental stability supporting the upward EPS revisions.

read.thesis: AMD demonstrates robust momentum with FY EPS estimates rising 11% over 90 days, supported by 39 upward revisions in the last month. This fundamental strength has attracted smart-money initiation from Leopold Aschenbrenner, heavy congressional buying, and a recent upgrade to Buy by Citigroup.
read.risk: The primary risk is that the 142% YTD run has stretched valuations, leaving the stock vulnerable to any signs of market share loss or slowing demand in the data center segment.
read.watch: Watch for the next earnings report, specifically data center revenue and forward guidance, to validate the aggressive upward revisions and Citigroup upgrade.

read.thesis: Exxon Mobil shows exceptional fundamental strength with FY EPS estimates surging 32% over 90 days, supported by a recent upgrade to Buy from B of A Securities. This momentum is backed by smart-money additions from Tom Gayner and net congressional buying, suggesting confidence in sustained cash flow generation.
read.risk: The bear case is that the aggressive EPS revisions are overly reliant on elevated commodity prices, making the stock vulnerable to a macroeconomic slowdown or energy price correction.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings for capital return updates and production guidance to confirm the fundamental strength driving the B of A upgrade and EPS revisions.

read.thesis: CCC Intelligent Solutions is trading at a significant 44% discount to its historical EV/EBITDA median, attracting smart-money additions from Chuck Akre. This deep value is supported by unanimous upward EPS revisions (10 up, 0 down in 30 days), suggesting the 30% YTD decline presents a dislocation from underlying fundamentals.
read.risk: The risk is that the historical multiple is no longer relevant due to slowing growth or increased competition in the auto claims software market, making the current valuation a trap.
read.watch: Watch for the next earnings print and forward guidance on revenue growth and margin expansion to confirm the fundamental stability supporting the upward revisions.

signals.label: 3 super-investors
signals.detail: Warren Buffett initiated; Seth Klarman added +0%; Chris Hohn added +0%

read.thesis: Alphabet exhibits strong fundamental momentum with FY EPS estimates rising 23% over 90 days, attracting high-profile initiations from Warren Buffett and additions from Klarman and Hohn. This institutional confidence is further supported by net congressional buying, indicating broad conviction in the company's core search and cloud businesses.
read.risk: The primary risk is that regulatory scrutiny or disruption from generative AI search alternatives could impair the core search monopoly, undermining the aggressive EPS revisions.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings for search revenue resilience and cloud growth metrics to validate the institutional conviction and upward EPS revisions.

signals.label: 2 super-investors
signals.detail: Daniel Loeb initiated; Eric Mandelblatt added +0%

read.thesis: Meta Platforms shows solid fundamental improvement with FY EPS estimates rising 11% over 90 days and unanimous upward revisions in the last month. This fundamental recovery has attracted smart-money initiation from Daniel Loeb and additions from Eric Mandelblatt, alongside net congressional buying.
read.risk: The bear case is that the fundamental improvements are driven by unsustainable cost-cutting rather than core revenue growth, leaving the stock vulnerable if ad spending slows.
read.watch: Watch for the next earnings report, specifically ad revenue growth and engagement metrics, to confirm that the upward EPS revisions are driven by top-line strength.

signals.label: 2 super-investors
signals.detail: Leopold Aschenbrenner initiated; Philippe Laffont initiated

read.thesis: Micron Technology demonstrates explosive fundamental momentum with FY EPS estimates surging 27% over 90 days, attracting smart-money initiations from Aschenbrenner and Laffont. This institutional conviction is supported by net congressional buying, suggesting confidence in a sustained memory cycle recovery despite the massive 250% YTD run.
read.risk: The primary risk is that the massive YTD outperformance has fully priced in the memory cycle recovery, making the stock highly susceptible to any signs of cyclical peaking or oversupply.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings for forward guidance on memory pricing and demand trends to confirm that the aggressive EPS revisions are sustainable.

signals.label: 4 super-investors
signals.detail: Seth Klarman initiated; Mason Morfit added +0%; Philippe Laffont initiated

read.thesis: Visa presents a compelling setup with smart-money initiations from Seth Klarman and Philippe Laffont, alongside additions from Mason Morfit. This strong institutional conviction is supported by net congressional buying and unanimous upward EPS revisions in the last 30 days, indicating confidence in the resilience of consumer spending.
read.risk: The strongest risk is that a macroeconomic slowdown or regulatory changes regarding swipe fees could impair payment volumes and margins, undermining the institutional conviction.
read.watch: Watch for the next earnings print and management commentary on cross-border volumes and consumer spending trends to validate the upward EPS revisions.

read.thesis: Mastercard offers a relative value opportunity, trading at a P/E 19% below its 10-year median, which has attracted smart-money additions from Eric Mandelblatt. This valuation discount is coupled with mixed but generally positive EPS revisions, suggesting the 6% YTD decline may present an attractive entry point for a high-quality compounder.
read.risk: The bear case is that the historical premium multiple is no longer justified due to slowing consumer spending or increased competition from alternative payment methods.
read.watch: Monitor upcoming earnings for payment volume growth and cross-border trends to confirm the fundamental stability supporting the value thesis.
